---
title: "Interview: Luca Gelisimo"
format:
  docx:
    toc: false
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: "margin=2.5cm"
    fontsize: 11pt
lang: nl
---

# Interview: Luca Gelisimo

## Metadata

| Veld | Waarde |
|---|---|
| Datum | 2026-05-18 |
| Collega | Luca Gelsomino |
| Rol | Onderzoeker |
| Gespreksvorm | Mondeling, één op één |
| Duur | ca. 25 minuten |

## Digitale toolkit

*Vul in vroeg in het gesprek. Alle tools, niet alleen AI.*

| Tool | Categorie | Waarvoor | Frequentie | Tevredenheid | Opmerking |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Clockify | Productiviteit | Tijdregistratie dagelijkse taken | Dagelijks | Positief | Gratis webapp; denkt als enige in team het te gebruiken |
| Google Mail | Communicatie | E-mail (extern/persoonlijk) | Dagelijks | Neutraal | |
| Outlook | Communicatie | E-mail Windesheim | Dagelijks | Neutraal | |
| Word | Documenten | Papers en proposals schrijven | Dagelijks | Positief | Meeste werk gaat hier naartoe |
| Excel | Documenten | Data en analyse | Regelmatig | Positief | |
| PowerPoint | Documenten | Presentaties | Regelmatig | Matig | Wil betere/geautomatiseerde slides |
| Mendeley | Literatuur | Wetenschappelijke papers beheren | Regelmatig | Positief | "Most useful one that I know" |
| Obsidian | Productiviteit | Notities, tracking | Regelmatig | Positief | Breed gebruik, geen specifieke workflow |
| Microsoft Teams | Communicatie | Samenwerking Windesheim | Dagelijks | Negatief | "I kind of hate it, but that's what it is" |
| Overleaf | Documenten | LaTeX samen met collega's | Regelmatig | Neutraal | |
| Sumatra PDF | Documenten | PDF lezen | Dagelijks | Positief | Overgestapt van Acrobat |
| ChatGPT | AI/LLM | Schrijven, Python code, JavaScript voor Qualtrics | Dagelijks | Positief | Gepersonaliseerd; code copy-pasten in VS Code |
| Draw.io | Ontwerp | Diagrammen (wat PowerPoint niet kan) | Regelmatig | Positief | Windesheim betaalt |
| Visual Studio Code | Ontwikkeling | Python uitvoeren | Dagelijks | Positief | |
| Stata | Analyse | Statistiek | Regelmatig | Positief | |
| Qualtrics | Onderzoek | Surveys | Regelmatig | Matig | Vereist JavaScript maatwerk; vroeger weken per taak |
| Refinitiv | Data | Jaarverslagen downloaden | Incidenteel | Matig | Geen API-toegang via Windesheim; handmatige batch-downloads |
| Claude | AI/LLM | Code via GitHub | Geprobeerd, gestopt | Negatief | GitHub-integratie werkte niet; na 2 frustraties gestopt |
| Krisp | AI/LLM | Vergadertranscriptie + notities | Overweging | Geinteresseerd | Collega gebruikt het; Luca overweegt voor eigen meetings en onderzoeksinterviews |

Categorieën: AI/LLM, AI lokaal, Automatisering, Literatuur, Projectmanagement, Ontwikkeling, Communicatie, Documenten, Andere

---

## Ruwe transcript

*Woordelijk verslag van het gesprek. Blijft privé in de repo. Niet gedeeld.*

**14:03-14:08 (toolkit)**
Luca: "Actually, the first thing I do is that I use an app called Clockify and I register how I spent my time the day before. That's free to use. The web app. And I'm pretty sure I'm the only one that uses it, but I find it very useful."
Luca: "I don't really check my mails in the morning, but eventually I will check my mails. I have to use two systems. Google Mail and Outlook for Windesheim. Then I usually use the office suite. Especially only Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, that's... I would say most of my work goes to either one of these three. I use a lot something called Mendeley for managing scientific papers."
Luca: "I use Obsidian for a lot of things, from taking notes to tracking stuff. I have to use Teams for Windesheim, even though I kind of hate it. I also use something called Overleaf. Which is a LaTeX... yeah, whatever you want to call it. I read a lot of PDFs. I moved away from Acrobat to something else. Sumatra. Just for opening PDFs."
Luca: "I use ChatGPT quite a lot for a lot of stuff nowadays. That's the main one I use in terms of AI, at least on a daily basis. Sometimes I use the others, but not that much. I use something called Draw IO. And that's because Windesheim pays for it. When basically I cannot do stuff with PowerPoint, I do it with Draw IO. I use Visual Studio Code for Python. I use Stata for statistical stuff."

**14:08-14:11 (schrijven + ChatGPT gebruik)**
Interviewer: "Considering what you're doing with these tools, what do you imagine that you are spending most of your time on?"
Luca: "Writing. It's either papers or proposals."
Interviewer: "And you don't use templates for this? You write them all from scratch?"
Luca: "Well, there's no really a template for writing a paper. Scientific papers do not have templates, but that doesn't mean I write them from scratch. It's a very complex process. It always involves multiple people, multiple versions of the same document."
Interviewer: "And you said you're using ChatGPT already. What do you find most useful about it?"
Luca: "I would say two things. The writing, I use it the most there. I don't ask ChatGPT to write stuff because it's pretty bad at it still, especially scientific stuff. But I have invested quite a lot of time into personalizing ChatGPT. So now it got much better, but it's still not that good. But what is very good at is... [substantial time saving]."

**14:12**
Luca: "And the second thing that I do with ChatGPT is Python code. I do not write any single line of Python code anymore. First of all, I'm not very good at it. And ChatGPT is fast and reliable. Also with JavaScript, so I use..."
Interviewer: "Yeah, yeah, that's pretty good."
Luca: "For surveys, for survey, I have to use something called Qualtrics. So from time to time, I have to use it extensively. Qualtrics requires a lot of JavaScript to do the things that you want it to do. And it used to be a pain in the ***, so I would invest like weeks to get like one thing done in JavaScript because I'm not that good at JavaScript. But nowadays, when Qualtrics doesn't do what I want, I just ask ChatGPT and usually in one, two or three times, it gives me the right JavaScript code that I can put into Qualtrics to get the exact survey that I want. So that's very useful. And then all the Python codes, Python coding, I do it through ChatGPT and it's extremely effective. It works very well. I tried with Claude because it used to have this thing connected to your GitHub account. It didn't work that well when I tried it. So at the second point, I got a bit frustrated and then moved back to ChatGPT. I have to copy paste the code myself, but still more effective than what Claude was doing with my GitHub."

**14:13-14:14**
Interviewer: "You have the ability, by the way, with ChatGPT, with a little plus at the bottom, to connect it directly to GitHub, right? You know this?"
Luca: "Yeah, okay. No, I didn't know it, but the workflow was also a bit clunky because I don't really give much care that things go into my GitHub. I want them to go into my virtual studio code. Which doesn't seem to be possible, at least to my knowledge. So what I do now, which is the fastest, is that I just prompt ChatGPT into giving me the Python code and then I drop it in Visual Studio and it usually works out."
Interviewer: "What would you describe as one of the more painful things that you have to do?"
Luca: "I get too many requests to do too much stuff. So I need some kind of heuristic to filter out requests. And I just assume that, okay, well, this is obviously a mass message. So they are not really seeking me out. So..."
Interviewer: "That's not personal enough."
Luca: "Yeah, they're not addressing me, so it doesn't matter whether I react or not. They are probably addressing hundreds of people, so if you get like that email... Join our conference. Yeah, okay, well, you obviously sent that to thousands of people, so you don't care that I joined your conference. So I don't invest time into understanding what the conference is about. On the other side, if someone reaches out to me and says, well, I have done... I've read this or I know you through this channel or whatever. And I think, would you like to come to this conference? Then at the very least, I acknowledge it or I try to understand what the conference is about."

**14:16-14:17 (pijnpunten vervolgd)**
Luca: "So we were saying painful things. I was saying writing stuff like literature reviews, not the actual reading, that's fine. But the actual writing of the review is extremely time-consuming and not that much fun. And proposal writing. I still have the impression that there's a lot that can be improved in terms of automation, but I don't have a good workflow."

**14:17-14:20 (stagiairvraag)**
Interviewer: "If you would have like an intern, and you could ask it almost anything you want, what would you ask it to do for you?"
Luca: "I would probably outsource data analysis because I think that that can be done reasonably well. And the annual report research, I'm pretty sure that could be easily outsourced to an intern. Making a survey, if you give it instruction, can be outsourced to an intern. I have masters students doing it, so I guess an intern can do it as well. Data collection. LinkedIn reach out, right? So we reach out to like thousands of people on LinkedIn, and that is basically mostly manual. But that can be automated for sure."

**14:20-14:22 (outreach)**
Luca: "It has to be automated in a way that is not easily noticeable because people don't reply to automated emails. I don't for sure. And no one else does. So if you write on LinkedIn, for example, you need to be sure that it's a human-like type of message. If it's a very marketing type of message, then people will just ignore you."
Interviewer: "Why don't you reply to automated emails? What's the emotion behind this?"
Luca: "Because I get too many requests. So I need some kind of heuristic to filter out requests. I just assume that, okay, well, this is obviously a mass message. So they are not really seeking me out. Join our conference. Yeah, okay, well, you obviously sent that to thousands of people, so you don't care that I joined your conference. On the other side, if someone reaches out to me and says, I've read this or I know you through this channel, and would you like to come to this conference? Then at the very least, I acknowledge it."

**14:22-14:27 (annual reports drill-down + PowerPoint)**
Interviewer: "You said every part of the data process was like screaming for automation?"
Luca: "The reach out and the reach out process. In relation to the event that we did in Munich, the reach out was the activity that took the most time. The rest was relatively straightforward."
Interviewer: "I'm more talking about the annual reports research."
Luca: "Annual reports. Yeah, that can be fully automated, I think. So what the way I currently do it: I downloaded a bunch of annual reports from a tool called Refinitiv. Which is already a problem, because we do not have the API to do the mass download. So I have to do like four or five batch downloads. After that, I had a script that extracted the data from the annual report and sent it into the API of ChatGPT and gave me back an evaluation of those extracted paragraphs based on criteria I had decided. A master student wrote the script. But the assessment procedure required a lot of fine tuning. I got it working, so I made the assessment of all the paragraphs. There were still errors, so I had to check all 200-300 paragraphs because I could not change the prompt to make it return close to 0 errors. So at a certain point I said, okay, it's more time effective to just go through all of them myself. After that I got the Excel file with the results, and then I had to do some analysis on that data. I was a bit out of time so I did it all myself. But that part I can also automate, because it's pretty mechanical."
Luca: "Wait, one thing. I also want to improve on making slides. I went into a meeting with a guy in Spain in supply chain management, and he had this awesome... But right now, ChatGPT is useless in this sense. It's complete garbage. But also the others, like I tried Claude and also did not return good PowerPoint slides. So if you know how to do PowerPoint slides that do not suck, then I'm extremely interested."

**14:29-14:30 (Krisp)**
Luca: "Another thing that I'm looking into seriously. One thing is to record meetings. So I saw one tool. Krisp with a K. And it's sort of geared towards meetings. I went into a couple of meetings with a guy for a project and this guy records all meetings with Krisp. So he basically opens the app on his phone and lets the app record the meeting. And after the meeting, it sends the transcription and a summary. It has more useful meeting notes. And I have to say, they are pretty useful. So I'm considering whether to... And I'm thinking also about some kind of tool for interviews, for research, in which you automatically get transcription and summary after the interview. That is also something that I would probably find very interesting. But there are a lot of issues with that. So for now it's a very low priority for me."

**14:33-14:40 (Quarto demo + afsluiting)**
*[Interviewer demonstreert Quarto: research proposal gegenereerd via Claude, PowerPoint vanuit tekstbestand, website.]*
Luca: "But how do you pull from the different AI? Is that through the API?"
Luca: "No, it sounds extremely interesting. Thanks a lot. Have a nice day."

## Gestructureerde Q&A

*Samenvatting per vraag op basis van het transcript. Blijft privé.*

### 1. Recente taak (opener)

> "Waar was je deze week het meest tijd aan kwijt?"

Antwoord: Meeste tijd gaat naar schrijven: papers en proposals. Niet vanuit een leeg document, maar een complex proces met meerdere mensen en versies. Literatuurreviews schrijven is extreem tijdrovend en niet leuk. Proposals: gevoel dat er veel te automatiseren valt maar nog geen goede workflow. Jaarverslagenanalyse eerder ook grote tijdsinvestering.

### 2. Drill-down

> "Je zei dat je [X] doet. Hoe begint dat precies, stap voor stap?"

Antwoord: Jaarverslagenanalyse (annual reports). Stappen: (1) Handmatige batch-downloads uit Refinitiv (geen API via Windesheim, 4-5 batches). (2) Python-script extraheert tekst uit PDFs. (3) Script stuurt paragrafen naar ChatGPT API voor evaluatie op criteria. (4) Veel fine-tuning nodig van prompt. (5) Uiteindelijk toch 200-300 paragrafen handmatig gecheckt omdat errors niet uit prompt te filteren waren. (6) Excel met resultaten. (7) Data-analyse handmatig gedaan wegens tijdgebrek. Conclusie: volledig automatiseerbaar in theorie, maar Refinitiv API-toegang ontbreekt en prompt-optimalisatie was flessenhals.

### 3. Workaround

> "Heb je voor [X] zelf een manier bedacht om het makkelijker te maken?"

Antwoord: Ja, twee sterke workarounds: (1) ChatGPT voor alle Python-code, output handmatig plakken in VS Code. Schrijft zelf geen enkele regel Python meer. (2) ChatGPT voor JavaScript in Qualtrics: vroeger weken per taak, nu 1-3 ChatGPT-prompts.

### 4. Volume

> "Hoeveel van dit soort [X] doe je per week?"

Antwoord: *Niet vastgelegd (transcript ontbreekt).*

### 5. AI-gebruik

> "Gebruik je zelf al AI-tools, voor werk of privé?"

Antwoord: Ja, ChatGPT actief voor: (1) schrijfhulp (gepersonaliseerd, maar vraagt het niet om te schrijven - wel om te verbeteren), (2) Python code, (3) JavaScript voor Qualtrics. Claude geprobeerd voor code via GitHub, gestopt na 2 frustraties. Overweegt Krisp voor vergader- en interviewtranscriptie (collega gebruikt het), maar privacybezwaren houden adoptie tegen. ChatGPT en Claude zijn "garbage" voor PowerPoint slides.

### 6. Eerder geprobeerd en gestopt?

> "Heb je ooit iets geprobeerd om [X] makkelijker te maken en ben je daarmee gestopt?"

Antwoord: Ja twee: (1) Claude voor code via GitHub, gestopt na twee frustraties. (2) Jaarverslagenanalyse gedeeltelijk geautomatiseerd maar te tijdrovend om te herhalen.

### 7. Kwaliteitscompromis

> "Wat doe je als je haast hebt? Wat laat je dan weg?"

Antwoord: Data-analyse uitbesteden aan intern/stagiair is realistisch. Annual report research kan volledig uitbesteed worden. Survey maken met instructies. Data collectie. LinkedIn outreach naar duizenden mensen (handmatig, "can be automated for sure" maar moet menselijk klinken anders geen reactie).

### 8. Samenwerking en schaal

> "Doe je [X] altijd alleen, of zijn er collega's die hetzelfde doen?"

Antwoord: *Niet vastgelegd (transcript ontbreekt).*

### 9. Foutkosten

> "Wat gaat er mis als [X] niet goed gaat?"

Antwoord: *Niet vastgelegd (transcript ontbreekt).*

### 10. Bestaande tools en systemen

> "Welke programma's of systemen gebruik je al bij [X]?"

Antwoord: ChatGPT + Visual Studio Code voor Python. Qualtrics voor surveys (JavaScript nodig). LaTeX bekend. Note AI gezien bij collega.

### 11. De magische stagiairvraag

> "Stel: er zit een slimme stagiair een week naast je. Wat laat je die als eerste doen?"

Antwoord: *Niet vastgelegd (transcript ontbreekt).*

### 12. Succescriterium

> "Stel het werkt. Hoe merk je dat het beter gaat?"

Antwoord: *Niet vastgelegd (transcript ontbreekt).*

## Kleine radartjes uit dit gesprek

| Werkproces | Klein radartje | Inputtype | Gewenste output | Privacyniveau | Trigger | Opmerking |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Werkproces | Klein radartje | Inputtype | Gewenste output | Privacyniveau | Trigger | Opmerking |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Python-code schrijven | ChatGPT genereert, handmatig in VS Code | Tekstprompt | Werkende Python-code | Intern | Herhaling, workaround | Al opgelost, maar workflow omslachtig |
| JavaScript voor Qualtrics | ChatGPT genereert JS voor surveys | Tekstprompt | Werkende Qualtrics-survey | Intern | Was weken, nu minuten | Sterk voorbeeld van AI als force multiplier |
| LinkedIn/e-mail outreach | Gepersonaliseerde berichten op schaal | Contactenlijst + profiel | Menselijk klinkende uitnodigingen | Persoonsgegevens | "Screaming for automation" | Hoog volume, handmatig; mag niet detecteerbaar geautomatiseerd zijn |
| Jaarverslagenanalyse | Volledige pijplijn automatiseren | PDF jaarverslagen via Refinitiv | Geëvalueerde paragrafen in Excel | Publiek | Te tijdrovend om te herhalen | Flessenhals: geen Refinitiv API + prompt-fouten vereisten 200-300 handmatige checks |
| PowerPoint slides maken | AI-gegenereerde slides met goede opmaak | Tekst/structuur | Professionele presentatie | Intern | "Complete garbage" bij ChatGPT en Claude | Collega in Spanje had indrukwekkende AI-slides |
| Vergader- en interviewtranscriptie | Krisp of vergelijkbaar | Audio | Transcriptie + samenvatting + notities | Persoonsgegevens | Gezien bij collega (Krisp) | Privacybezwaren remmen adoptie |
| Papers en proposals schrijven | AI-ondersteund reviewen en verbeteren | Concept tekst | Verbeterde versie | Intern | Traag, complex, meerdere mensen | Vraagt ChatGPT niet om te schrijven, wel om te verbeteren |

## Letterlijke signalen

- "I do not write any single line of Python code anymore."
- "It used to be a pain in the ***, so I would invest like weeks to get like one thing done in JavaScript."
- "The reach out was the activity that took the most time." (over Munich-event)
- "Every part of the data process was screaming for automation."
- "I cannot really do it again, even though I automated it partially, but it was still painful."
- "It has to be automated in a way that is not easily noticeable because people don't reply to automated emails."
- "Right now, ChatGPT is useless in this sense. It's complete garbage." (over PowerPoint)
- "I do not ask ChatGPT to write stuff because it's pretty bad at it still, especially scientific stuff."

## Nog niet oplossen

Noteer hier ideeën die opkomen, maar nog niet als oplossing worden voorgesteld.

- Directe VS Code-integratie met AI (Copilot, Continue.dev)
- Whisper lokaal voor interviewtranscriptie (GDPR-proof alternatief voor Krisp)
- Elicit of ASReview voor literatuurreview
- Outreach-automatisering met gepersonaliseerde berichttemplates
- Refinitiv API-toegang regelen via Windesheim voor jaarverslagenpijplijn
- Quarto PowerPoint template voor het team

---

## Samenvatting voor weekupdate

*Dit blok is het enige dat gedeeld wordt. Geen namen, geen ruwe notities.*

**Rol/context collega:** Onderzoeker

**In het kort:** Pragmatische AI-gebruiker die ChatGPT al intensief inzet voor code (Python en JavaScript) en schrijfverbetering. Grootste onopgeloste pijnpunten zijn outreach op schaal, jaarverslagenanalyse (gedeeltelijk geautomatiseerd maar niet herhaalbaar), en het ontbreken van een goede AI-oplossing voor slides. Overweegt vergader- en interviewtranscriptie via Krisp maar privacyvraag blokkeert.

**Kleine radartjes:**

- LinkedIn/e-mail outreach op schaal: hoog volume, handmatig, "screaming for automation", maar moet menselijk klinken
- Jaarverslagenanalyse: gedeeltelijk geautomatiseerd, flessenhals is Refinitiv API-toegang en prompt-kwaliteit
- PowerPoint slides: actieve frustratie, collega in Spanje had indrukwekkende AI-slides, ChatGPT en Claude schieten tekort
- Vergadertranscriptie: interesse in Krisp, geblokkeerd door privacy

**Al in gebruik:** ChatGPT (code + schrijfverbetering), Visual Studio Code, Qualtrics, Stata, Mendeley, Obsidian, Clockify, Overleaf

**Sterkste signaal:** "It has to be automated in a way that is not easily noticeable because people don't reply to automated emails."
